serving.natural_language_portion: One large cup of iced homemade barley drink with lemon, approximately 480 ml, as typically served at Singaporean kopitiam chains such as Toast Box.

nutrient_source.title: Barley water, sweetened — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central Barley Water reference; composition estimated from pearl barley decoction with sugar and lemon juice
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Foodo entry for iced homemade barley water with lemon (large). Values estimated from HPB Singapore barley water (sweetened) reference and USDA FoodData Central pearl barley decoction data. A typical homemade barley drink is a dilute aqueous extract of pearl barley with added sugar (~5–8 g sugar per 100 ml) and a small amount of lemon juice. Per-100g values reflect a lightly sweetened barley water at approximately 25–30 kcal/100 ml, consistent with HPB and USDA reference data for sweetened grain-based beverages.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of barley-based beverages and grain decoctions — analogous estimate based on pearl barley GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for iced homemade barley water with lemon. Pearl barley has a well-established low GI of approximately 25–35 (University of Sydney GI Database). A sweetened barley water decoction is a dilute preparation; the GI is estimated at 30 (low category) based on the barley grain GI and the dilution effect. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (30 × 29.8) / 100 ≈ 8.9, where carbohydrate per 480 g serving = 6.2 × 4.8 = 29.76 g ≈ 29.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~3 mg/100 ml) and no added salt
hypertension.guidance: Iced barley water with lemon is very low in sodium (~14 mg per large 480 ml serving) and is generally safe for individuals managing hypertension, though the added sugar content should be monitored if overall caloric intake is a concern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar contributing approximately 28 g of sugar per large serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although barley itself has a low glycemic index (~30), the added sugar in a sweetened barley water drink contributes approximately 28 g of free sugars per large 480 ml serving, which can raise postprandial blood glucose; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request reduced-sugar or unsweetened versions and limit portion size.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; beta-glucan from barley may offer modest cardiovascular benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Iced barley water is essentially fat-free and cholesterol-free, and the beta-glucan soluble fibre from pearl barley has been associated with modest LDL-cholesterol reduction; however, the dilute preparation limits the therapeutic dose of beta-glucan per serving.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Homemade Barley Drink with Lemon (Large) is a traditional Singaporean cold beverage made by simmering pearl barley (薏米, yi mi) in water, sweetening with sugar, and adding a squeeze of fresh lemon juice before serving over ice. It is a staple offering at Singaporean kopitiam chains such as Toast Box and is widely consumed as a refreshing, mildly sweet grain-based drink. The drink has a pale, slightly cloudy appearance with a delicate starchy sweetness balanced by the tartness of lemon. The texture is thin and watery, similar to a lightly sweetened herbal water. Pearl barley is valued in traditional Chinese and Southeast Asian food culture for its perceived cooling and detoxifying properties. Nutritionally, a large 480 ml serving provides approximately 130 kcal, predominantly from added sugar (~28 g), with minimal protein (under 1 g) and negligible fat. The barley decoction contributes small amounts of B vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, and soluble beta-glucan fibre, though at dilute concentrations. The glycemic index is estimated at 30 (low), but the glycemic load per large serving is moderate (~8.9) due to the sugar content. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, a reduced-sugar or unsweetened version is advisable. The drink is naturally low in sodium, making it suitable for hypertension management. It is vegan, dairy-free, and halal-certified in most kopitiam contexts, but contains gluten from barley and is not suitable for those with coeliac disease or gluten intolerance.

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central entry exists for iced homemade barley water with lemon as served at Toast Box. Nutrient values are estimated from HPB Singapore barley water (sweetened) reference data and USDA pearl barley decoction data. GI is an analogous estimate based on pearl barley GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the food is a simple dilute grain decoction with well-characterised ingredients and the estimation basis is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from HPB Singapore Foodo barley water (sweetened) reference and USDA FoodData Central pearl barley data; no exact commercial product data is available for Toast Box large barley lemon drink. 2. Serving size of 480 g (ml) is estimated for a large cup at a Singaporean kopitiam chain; actual volume may vary by outlet. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources for this beverage type. 4. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources for this beverage type. 5. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources for this beverage type. 6. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources for this beverage type. 7. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources for this beverage type. 8. vitamin_b12_mcg is confirmed zero as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero as this beverage contains no vitamin D sources. 10. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are confirmed zero or trace as this is an essentially fat-free grain water beverage; fat_g of 0.1 reflects trace lipids from barley. 11. GI set to 30 (low category) as an analogous estimate based on pearl barley GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific preparation. 12. The food contains gluten from barley and is not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or gluten sensitivity. 13. Sugar content reflects typical sweetened kopitiam barley water; unsweetened versions would have significantly lower carbohydrate and energy values.
